expectations.text: Output uses Slack-style bold (*word*) rather than markdown ## headers
expectations.evidence: Uses **double-asterisk** bold throughout (e.g. **#platform-updates**, **Claude Code improvements**). Double asterisks don't render as bold in Slack — they show as literal asterisks or behave inconsistently.

expectations.text: Output contains at least one emoji section header (✅, 🔄, or 👀)
expectations.evidence: Uses text section headers like **Claude Code improvements**, **WezTerm terminal**, **Minor**. No emoji headers present.

expectations.text: Output references specific git activity (real commit, feature, or fix — not generic placeholder text)
expectations.evidence: References Playwright screenshots for PRs, sandbox TLS fix, vault read permissions, OSC 9 notification, WezTerm CWD fix, Obsidian note fix — real specifics.

expectations.text: Output does not begin with meta-commentary like 'I'll write' or 'Here is your update'
expectations.evidence: Second line reads 'Here's what shipped in the personal dev tooling this week.' — meta-commentary before the content begins.
